parsed_candidate.joint_topic: Test-Time Weight Updates as Cellular Competency for Coherent Inference
parsed_candidate.thesis: Treating per-token inner-model weight updates as agential cells that solve local prediction errors will produce more coherent global model behavior, where test-time training acts as a morphogenetic process that transforms local errors into globally consistent outputs.
parsed_candidate.mechanism: Test-time training updates a small inner model on each input sequence, which transforms local prediction errors into corrective weight adjustments; treating each updated sub-model as a competent agent routes these local corrections into a coherent global representation, analogous to how cells transform local errors into coherent anatomy.

prompt:
You are merging two research "atoms" into ONE candidate paradigm-shift research niche in AI / LLMs.

ATOM A (id ML_E1_A03, source arxiv): "Test-time training updates a small inner model on each input sequence, so the network adapts its own weights during inference rather than only at train time."
ATOM B (id ARXIV_R10_evodevo, source arxiv): "Evo-devo competency architectures treat cells as agents that solve morphogenetic problems, transforming local errors into coherent global anatomy."

Combine the mechanism in ATOM A with the mechanism/principle in ATOM B into a single, concrete, testable research direction.

Output ONLY a fenced ```json block (no prose before or after) with EXACTLY these fields:
{
  "joint_topic": "<=12 word title of the merged niche",
  "thesis": "1-2 sentence concrete research thesis",
  "mechanism": "1-2 sentences describing the causal mechanism; MUST use concrete causal verbs such as induces, produces, causes, transforms, regulates, activates, inhibits, routes",
  "operator": "a short relation label such as INDUCES or TRANSFORMS; do NOT use ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OPEN",
  "primary_quote": "a quote of at least 30 characters copied EXACTLY (verbatim, character-for-character) from ATOM A's text or ATOM B's text; do not paraphrase or invent",
  "verification_plan": [{"source": "<name>", "query": "<search query>"}]
}

Rules:
- primary_quote MUST be an exact substring of one atom's text. Copy it; do not invent.
- verification_plan MUST list at least 5 entries with 5 DISTINCT source names.
- Output nothing except the json block.
